revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23984597483|manual triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ceb:Gogo-shima]][[ja:興居島]] {{Infobox 島 | 島名=Gogoshima | 画像=[[File:Gogoshima 2019.jpg|290px]]
Aerial view of Gogoshima (from the northwest). The mountain on the right is Iyo-Kofuji. | 緯度度 = 33 |緯度分 = 54 |緯度秒 = 0 |N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 132 |経度分 = 40 |経度秒 = 10 |E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP-38 | 面積=9.27 | 周囲= | 標高=282 | 海域={{ill|Seto Inland Sea|en|Seto Inland Sea|qid=Q231312|lt=Seto Inland Sea|1=Seto Inland Sea}} | 国={{JPN}} {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|en|Ehime Prefecture|qid=Q123376|lt=Ehime Prefecture|1=Ehime Prefecture}}{{ill|Matsuyama|en|Matsuyama|qid=Q200022|lt=Matsuyama|1=Matsuyama}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Ehime Prefecture#Japan Matsuyama city#Japan|relief=1|float=center|label=Gogoshima}} |OSMズーム = 11 }} '''Gogoshima''' (興居島) is the second-largest island in the {{ill|Kutsuna Islands|ja|忽那諸島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsuna Islands|1=Kutsuna Islands|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q8203553}}, located in the western {{ill|Seto Inland Sea|en|Seto Inland Sea|qid=Q231312|lt=Seto Inland Sea|1=Seto Inland Sea}}.[https://www.pref.ehime.jp/site/chuyo/4779.html 島の魅力 興居島] [[Ehime Prefectural Office]] 中予地方局 homepage (accessed September 21, 2024). It has an area of 9.27 square kilometers and is part of {{ill|Matsuyama|en|Matsuyama|qid=Q200022|lt=Matsuyama|1=Matsuyama}} in {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|en|Ehime Prefecture|qid=Q123376|lt=Ehime Prefecture|1=Ehime Prefecture}}, located about 2 kilometers offshore to the west of {{ill|Takamatsu Port|ja|松山港|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamatsu Port|1=Port of Matsuyama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11529892}}. The island is connected to the mainland by regular ferry service.[https://www.sankei.com/article/20240920-OB6H3Z24JVPAHPTG77YUYOB7HY/ 小林希［島を歩く日本を見る］興居島（松山市） 千年続く伝承、島つなぐ人の想い]『{{ill|Sankei Shimbun|en|Sankei Shimbun|qid=Q1197261|lt=Sankei Shimbun|1=Sankei Shimbun}}』朝刊2024th year 9月20日（生活面） It is a designated inhabited remote island. == Geography == The island's northern tip features {{ill|Koto-Hikibana|ja|琴引鼻|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koto-Hikibana|1=Cape Kotohiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11573779}}, and it is separated by the {{ill|Tsurushima Strait|ja|釣島海峡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurushima Strait|1=Tsurushima Strait|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11648226}} from {{ill|Nakajima (Ehime)|ja|中島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakajima (Ehime)|1=Nakashima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1963981}} and {{ill|Mutsukijima|ja|睦月島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutsukijima|1=Muzuki Island|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11583780}} to the north. The surrounding area includes other islands in the {{ill|Kutsuna Islands|ja|忽那諸島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsuna Islands|1=Kutsuna Islands|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q8203553}}. To the west lies {{ill|Tsurushima|ja|釣島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurushima|1=Tsurushima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11648224}}, an inhabited satellite island, while the southwest opens to {{ill|Iyo-Nada|ja|伊予灘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo-Nada|1=Iyo Sea|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11378825}}. The Tsurushima Strait serves as a navigational route connecting the eastern {{ill|Saikaido|ja|斎灘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saikaido|1=Itsuki Sea|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11500419}} and western Iyo-Nada, and it is also a branching point for ships heading towards {{ill|Hiroshima Bay|en|Hiroshima Bay|qid=Q114575|lt=Hiroshima Bay|1=Hiroshima Bay}}. The strait has witnessed several maritime accidents. The island has a distinctive hourglass shape, with a narrowest width of about 200 meters in a location named Funakoshi. The northern part of the island is gently sloping, with hilly terrain, while the southern part is marked by {{ill|Iyo-Kofuji|ja|伊予小富士|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo-Kofuji|1=Iyo Kofuji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11378816}} (282.4 m),{{Cite journal|和書|author=佐藤剛, 木村誇, 廣田清治, 鄒青穎, 八木浩司 |date=2019 |url=https://doi.org/10.3313/jls.56.129 |title=平成30th year 7月豪雨によって発生した愛媛県興居島の崩壊分布と土砂移動プロセス |journal=日本地すべり学会誌 |ISSN=13483986 |publisher=日本地すべり学会 |volume=56 |issue=3 |pages=129-134 |doi=10.3313/jls.56.129 |naid=130007661068 |accessdate=2023-05-09}} a landmark that also acts as a natural breakwater for Takahama Port. === Land Use === Forests cover the mountain tops, while slopes are used for agriculture, particularly the cultivation of onions and fruit.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://udcm.jp/wp-content/uploads/2022/03/0de9473ee996e461db1767c03c679016.pdf|title=4.2 興居島|publisher=Matsuyama Urban Design Center|accessdate=2022-11-03}} The island is renowned for its citrus production, including mandarins, earning it the nickname "Fruit Island."Before citrus cultivation, peaches and loquats were grown, starting in the Edo and Meiji periods, respectively. Citrus later became the dominant crop due to its suitability for the island's climate and soil. === Natural Features === ==== Geology ==== The island is primarily composed of Late Cretaceous granodiorite, with some intrusions of Miocene andesite, which forms volcanic necks like Iyo-Kofuji. ==== Flora and Fauna ==== Four species of antlions have been recorded on the island.山内健生・松野茂富「四国西部の島嶼におけるヒロバカゲロウ科，ウスバカゲロウ科，ツノトンボ科の記録」『四国自然史科学研究』11: 59-61（2018th year ）https://www.jstage.jst.go.jp/article/sinh/11/0/11_59/_article/-char/ja/ == Society == === Population === As of the 2015 census, the population was 1,141 across 587 households. === Administration === The entire island belongs to Matsuyama City. It was incorporated into the city on February 1, 1954, following the merger of {{ill|Gogoshima Village|ja|興居島村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gogoshima Village|1=Gogoshima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11613385}} in {{ill|Onsen District, Ehime|en|Onsen District, Ehime|qid=Q1515027|lt=Onsen District|1=Onsen District, Ehime}}. === Education === Previously, two elementary schools, Yura Elementary and Tomari Elementary, operated on the island. They were merged into Matsuyama Municipal Gogoshima Elementary School in April 2009, which also became part of the integrated elementary and middle school, {{ill|Matsuyama Municipal Gogoshima Elementary and Middle School|ja|松山市立興居島小中学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuyama Municipal Gogoshima Elementary and Middle School|1=Matsuyama Municipal Gogoshima Elementary and Middle School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11529846}}. A branch school on Tsurushima was closed in 2013. == History == The island served as a military base in 1465 during the {{ill|Ōnin War|en|Ōnin War|qid=Q385676|lt=Ōnin War|1=Ōnin War}}, where {{ill|Ōuchi Norihiro|ja|大内教弘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōuchi Norihiro|1=Ōuchi Norihiro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11432532}} passed away while supporting Kōno Michiharu. During the Edo period, the island belonged to the {{ill|Matsuyama Domain|ja|松山藩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuyama Domain|1=Q64578026|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q64578026}}. == Economy == The main industries are agriculture and fishing. Citrus cultivation dominates agriculture, while aquaculture includes pearl and worm farming. == Culture == The island hosts traditional "Boat Dancing," a nationally recognized intangible cultural property. == Transport == Ferries operated by {{ill|Gogoshima Co.|ja|株式会社ごごしま|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gogoshima Co.|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} connect the island's ports, Yura and Tomari, to Takahama Port in Matsuyama.ごごしま公式サイト内：[https://gogoshima-ferry.com/timetable_faretable/　由良高浜航路／泊高浜航路][[時刻表]]（2024th year 9月21日閲覧） == Notable People == * {{ill|Akikazu Nishimoto|ja|西本明和|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akikazu Nishimoto|1=Akikazu Nishimoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11628471}}, professional baseball player * {{ill|Takashi Nishimoto|en|Takashi Nishimoto|qid=Q8010225|lt=Tadashi Nishimoto|1=Takashi Nishimoto}}, professional baseball player and younger brother of Akikazu Nishimoto == References == {{Reflist}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Gogoshima}} * [http://island-matsuyama.com/shimanosyoukai.html#gogoshima Gogoshima (Matsuyama Remote Islands Promotion Association)] * [http://gogoshima-ferry.com/ Gogoshima Co. Homepage] {{translated page|ja|興居島|version=103402063|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ceb:Gogo-shima]] [[ja:興居島]] {{Commons category|Gogoshima}} {{wikidata link|Q11613384}} [[Category:Geography of Matsuyama]] [[Category:Islands of Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Islands of the Seto Inland Sea]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
